detaileddescription.para:
Get current video height. Obsolète
Use libvlc_video_get_size() instead.

simplesect.para: the video pixel height or 0 if not applicable

detaileddescription.para:
Get current video width. Obsolète
Use libvlc_video_get_size() instead.

simplesect.para: the video pixel width or 0 if not applicable

detaileddescription.para: Get the description of available titles.

simplesect.para: list containing description of available titles. It must be freed with libvlc_track_description_list_release()

detaileddescription.para: Get the description of available chapters for specific title.

simplesect.para: list containing description of available chapter for title i_title. It must be freed with libvlc_track_description_list_release()

detaileddescription.para:
Set new video subtitle file. Obsolète
Use libvlc_media_player_add_slave() instead.

simplesect.para: the success status (boolean)

detaileddescription.para:
Toggle teletext transparent status on video output. Obsolète
use libvlc_video_set_teletext() instead.
string argument, "file,d,t;file,d,t;..."
option values for libvlc_video_{get,set}_logo_{int,string}
option values for libvlc_video_{get,set}_adjust_{int,float,bool}
Toggle fullscreen status on non-embedded video outputs.

simplesect.para: The same limitations applies to this function as to libvlc_set_fullscreen().

detaileddescription.para: Enable or disable fullscreen.

simplesect.para: With most window managers, only a top-level windows can be in full-screen mode. Hence, this function will not operate properly if libvlc_media_player_set_xwindow() was used to embed the video in a non-top-level window. In that case, the embedding window must be reparented to the root window before fullscreen mode is enabled. You will want to reparent it back to its normal parent when disabling fullscreen.

detaileddescription.para: Get current fullscreen status.

p_mi
the media player
the fullscreen status (boolean)
\libvlc_return_bool
Enable or disable key press events handling, according to the LibVLC hotkeys configuration. By default and for historical reasons, keyboard events are handled by the LibVLC video widget.

simplesect.para: On X11, there can be only one subscriber for key press and mouse click events per window. If your application has subscribed to those events for the X window ID of the video widget, then LibVLC will not be able to handle key presses and mouse clicks in any case.
This function is only implemented for X11 and Win32 at the moment.

parameterdescription.para: true to handle key press events, false to ignore them.

detaileddescription.para: Enable or disable mouse click events handling. By default, those events are handled. This is needed for DVD menus to work, as well as a few video filters such as "puzzle".

simplesect.para: libvlc_video_set_key_input().
This function is only implemented for X11 and Win32 at the moment.

parameterdescription.para: true to handle mouse click events, false to ignore them.

detaileddescription.para: Get the pixel dimensions of a video.

parameterdescription.para: number of the video (starting from, and most commonly 0)
pointer to get the pixel width [OUT]
pointer to get the pixel height [OUT]

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if the specified video does not exist

detaileddescription.para: Get the mouse pointer coordinates over a video. Coordinates are expressed in terms of the decoded video resolution, not in terms of pixels on the screen/viewport (to get the latter, you can query your windowing system directly).
Either of the coordinates may be negative or larger than the corresponding dimension of the video, if the cursor is outside the rendering area.

simplesect.para: The coordinates may be out-of-date if the pointer is not located on the video rendering area. LibVLC does not track the pointer if it is outside of the video widget.
LibVLC does not support multiple pointers (it does of course support multiple input devices sharing the same pointer) at the moment.

parameterdescription.para: number of the video (starting from, and most commonly 0)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if the specified video does not exist

detaileddescription.para: Get the current video scaling factor. See also libvlc_video_set_scale().

simplesect.para: the currently configured zoom factor, or 0. if the video is set to fit to the output window/drawable automatically.

detaileddescription.para: Set the video scaling factor. That is the ratio of the number of pixels on screen to the number of pixels in the original decoded video in each dimension. Zero is a special value; it will adjust the video to the output window/drawable (in windowed mode) or the entire screen.
Note that not all video outputs support scaling.
Get current video aspect ratio.

simplesect.para: the video aspect ratio or NULL if unspecified (the result must be released with free() or libvlc_free()).

detaileddescription.para: Set new video aspect ratio.

parameterdescription.para: new video aspect-ratio or NULL to reset to default

simplesect.para: Invalid aspect ratios are ignored.

detaileddescription.para: Create a video viewpoint structure.

simplesect.para: LibVLC 3.0.0 and later
video viewpoint or NULL (the result must be released with free() or libvlc_free()).

detaileddescription.para: Update the video viewpoint information.

simplesect.para: It is safe to call this function before the media player is started.
LibVLC 3.0.0 and later

parameterdescription.para: if true replace the old viewpoint with the new one. If false, increase/decrease it.

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error, 0 otherwise
the values are set asynchronously, it will be used by the next frame displayed.

detaileddescription.para: Get current video subtitle.

simplesect.para: the video subtitle selected, or -1 if none

detaileddescription.para: Get the number of available video subtitles.

simplesect.para: the number of available video subtitles

detaileddescription.para: Get the description of available video subtitles.

simplesect.para: list containing description of available video subtitles. It must be freed with libvlc_track_description_list_release()

detaileddescription.para: Set new video subtitle.

parameterdescription.para: video subtitle track to select (i_id from track description)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if out of range

detaileddescription.para: Get the current subtitle delay. Positive values means subtitles are being displayed later, negative values earlier.

simplesect.para: time (in microseconds) the display of subtitles is being delayed
LibVLC 2.0.0 or later

detaileddescription.para: Set the subtitle delay. This affects the timing of when the subtitle will be displayed. Positive values result in subtitles being displayed later, while negative values will result in subtitles being displayed earlier.
The subtitle delay will be reset to zero each time the media changes.

parameterdescription.para: time (in microseconds) the display of subtitles should be delayed

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 on error
LibVLC 2.0.0 or later

detaileddescription.para: Get the full description of available titles

simplesect.para: LibVLC 3.0.0 and later.

parameterdescription.para: address to store an allocated array of title descriptions descriptions (must be freed with libvlc_title_descriptions_release() by the caller) [OUT]

simplesect.para: the number of titles (-1 on error)

detaileddescription.para: Release a title description

simplesect.para: LibVLC 3.0.0 and later

parameterdescription.para: number of title descriptions to release

detaileddescription.para: Get the full description of available chapters

simplesect.para: LibVLC 3.0.0 and later.

parameterdescription.para: index of the title to query for chapters (uses current title if set to -1)
address to store an allocated array of chapter descriptions descriptions (must be freed with libvlc_chapter_descriptions_release() by the caller) [OUT]

simplesect.para: the number of chapters (-1 on error)

detaileddescription.para: Release a chapter description

simplesect.para: LibVLC 3.0.0 and later

parameterdescription.para: number of chapter descriptions to release

detaileddescription.para: Get current crop filter geometry.

simplesect.para: the crop filter geometry or NULL if unset

detaileddescription.para: Set new crop filter geometry.

parameterdescription.para: new crop filter geometry (NULL to unset)

detaileddescription.para: Get current teletext page requested or 0 if it's disabled.
Teletext is disabled by default, call libvlc_video_set_teletext() to enable it.

simplesect.para: the current teletext page requested.

detaileddescription.para: Set new teletext page to retrieve.
This function can also be used to send a teletext key.

parameterdescription.para: teletex page number requested. This value can be 0 to disable teletext, a number in the range ]0;1000[ to show the requested page, or a libvlc_teletext_key_t. 100 is the default teletext page.

detaileddescription.para: Get number of available video tracks.

simplesect.para: the number of available video tracks (int)

detaileddescription.para: Get the description of available video tracks.

simplesect.para: list with description of available video tracks, or NULL on error. It must be freed with libvlc_track_description_list_release()

detaileddescription.para: Get current video track.

simplesect.para: the video track ID (int) or -1 if no active input

detaileddescription.para: Set video track.

parameterdescription.para: the track ID (i_id field from track description)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if out of range

memberdef.argsstring: (libvlc_media_player_t *p_mi, unsigned num, const char *psz_filepath, unsigned int i_width, unsigned int i_height)
memberdef.name: libvlc_video_take_snapshot

detaileddescription.para: Take a snapshot of the current video window.
If i_width AND i_height is 0, original size is used. If i_width XOR i_height is 0, original aspect-ratio is preserved.

parameterdescription.para: number of video output (typically 0 for the first/only one)
the path of a file or a folder to save the screenshot into

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if the video was not found

detaileddescription.para: Enable or disable deinterlace filter

parameterdescription.para: type of deinterlace filter, NULL to disable

detaileddescription.para: Get an integer marquee option value

simplesect.para: libvlc_video_marquee_int_option_t

detaileddescription.para: Get a string marquee option value

simplesect.para: libvlc_video_marquee_string_option_t

detaileddescription.para: Enable, disable or set an integer marquee option
Setting libvlc_marquee_Enable has the side effect of enabling (arg !0) or disabling (arg 0) the marq filter.

simplesect.para: libvlc_video_marquee_int_option_t

detaileddescription.para: Set a marquee string option

simplesect.para: libvlc_video_marquee_string_option_t

detaileddescription.para: Get integer logo option.

parameterdescription.para: logo option to get, values of libvlc_video_logo_option_t

detaileddescription.para: Set logo option as integer. Options that take a different type value are ignored. Passing libvlc_logo_enable as option value has the side effect of starting (arg !0) or stopping (arg 0) the logo filter.

parameterdescription.para: logo option to set, values of libvlc_video_logo_option_t

detaileddescription.para: Set logo option as string. Options that take a different type value are ignored.

parameterdescription.para: logo option to set, values of libvlc_video_logo_option_t

detaileddescription.para: Get integer adjust option.

parameterdescription.para: adjust option to get, values of libvlc_video_adjust_option_t

simplesect.para: LibVLC 1.1.1 and later.

detaileddescription.para: Set adjust option as integer. Options that take a different type value are ignored. Passing libvlc_adjust_enable as option value has the side effect of starting (arg !0) or stopping (arg 0) the adjust filter.

parameterdescription.para: adust option to set, values of libvlc_video_adjust_option_t

simplesect.para: LibVLC 1.1.1 and later.

detaileddescription.para: Get float adjust option.

parameterdescription.para: adjust option to get, values of libvlc_video_adjust_option_t

simplesect.para: LibVLC 1.1.1 and later.

detaileddescription.para: Set adjust option as float. Options that take a different type value are ignored.

parameterdescription.para: adust option to set, values of libvlc_video_adjust_option_t

simplesect.para: LibVLC 1.1.1 and later.
